text_blocks.text: NASA awarded all 32 of these astronauts its highest honor, the
Distinguished Service Medal, given for "distinguished service, ability, or
coverage", and personal "contribution representing substantial progress to the
NASA mission". The medals were awarded posthumously to Grissom,
White, and Chaffee in 1969, then to the crews of all missions from
Apollo 8 onward. The crew that flew the first Earth orbital test mission
Apollo 7, Walter M. Schirra, Donn Eisele, and Walter Cunningham, were
awarded the lesser NASA Exceptional Service Medal, because of discipline
problems with the Flight Director's orders during their flight. The NASA
Administrator in October, 2008, decided to award them the Distinguished
Service Medals, by this time posthumously to Schirra and Eisele.
